content.verdict: Airwallex is built for multi-currency, cross-border money movement — 20+ currencies, transfers to 200+ countries, and local payment methods on invoices — but it charges card processing fees (2.80%-4.30% + $0.30) on every plan. Mercury is a US-focused free bank account with no fees on core checking, USD wires, and AI-assisted bill pay, but treasury yield above 3% requires a $250K+ balance and it doesn't advertise multi-currency accounts. The choice comes down to whether the business's money moves across borders or stays in USD.
content.bestForToolA: Businesses receiving or paying in multiple currencies, running international transfers to 200+ countries, or needing local payment methods on invoices — Airwallex's Explore plan is free and includes global accounts in 20+ currencies plus interbank FX rates.
content.bestForToolB: US startups that want simple, fee-free checking and savings with no minimum balance, free USD wires, and accounting syncs to QuickBooks, Xero, or NetSuite without paying per-user platform fees.
content.whoNeedsBoth: Companies banking primarily in USD but transacting internationally may keep Mercury as their core US operating account while using Airwallex for foreign-currency collections, FX conversion, and paying overseas vendors or contractors.

keyDifferences.title: Multi-currency vs. USD-first banking
keyDifferences.toolA: Global Accounts support 20+ currencies with transfers to 200+ countries at interbank FX rates.
keyDifferences.toolB: Free USD wires with no stated multi-currency account support in the facts provided.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: A business collecting revenue or paying suppliers in foreign currencies avoids repeated conversion costs and delays with a true multi-currency account.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Companies with international customers, suppliers, or remote teams paid in local currencies.

keyDifferences.title: Transaction fees on cards vs. free core banking
keyDifferences.toolA: Card processing fees apply on all plans: 2.80% + $0.30 domestic, 4.30% + $0.30 international.
keyDifferences.toolB: No minimum balances, no monthly fees, and free ACH, wire, and check payments on the core plan.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Airwallex's fee structure reflects a payments/FX business model, while Mercury's core banking is genuinely free — the right pick depends on whether the primary need is payment acceptance or basic deposit banking.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Cost-sensitive startups comparing all-in banking costs versus businesses that need to accept card payments globally.

keyDifferences.title: AI automation scope
keyDifferences.toolA: AI Expense Policy Agent enforces spend policy across entities and currencies, but it's gated to the paid Grow plan ($12/user/month + platform fee).
keyDifferences.toolB: AI Bill Pay reads and populates bill details and categorizes transactions, described as part of the free bill pay feature set.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams wanting AI-driven automation without an added per-user fee may find Mercury's approach more accessible out of the gate.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Finance teams evaluating how much AI automation costs to unlock.

keyDifferences.title: Treasury / yield access
keyDifferences.toolA: Yield on USD balances scales with plan tier: 3.15% (Explore), 3.30% (Grow), 3.42% (Accelerate) — available without a balance minimum stated in the facts.
keyDifferences.toolB: Treasury yield up to 3.64% is offered via Mercury Advisory, but requires a $250K+ balance.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Airwallex's yield tiers are tied to plan level, while Mercury's higher yield is gated by account size rather than plan.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Startups with smaller balances who still want yield versus larger, well-capitalized companies that can meet Mercury's $250K threshold.

keyDifferences.title: Pricing structure: per-user vs. flat monthly fee
keyDifferences.toolA: Grow plan costs $12/user/month plus a platform fee, so cost scales with headcount using Spend cards.
keyDifferences.toolB: Mercury Plus is a flat $29.90/month regardless of user count.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Growing teams should model whether per-user or flat pricing is cheaper at their headcount.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Finance leads budgeting for scaling teams.

rows.note: Airwallex supports 20+ currencies
Mercury offers free USD wires; broader international transfer support isn't documented
Airwallex invoicing supports 160+ local payment methods
Airwallex's AI agent requires the Grow plan; Mercury's AI bill pay is part of core banking
Both sync with Xero, QuickBooks, and NetSuite
Mercury's higher yield requires a $250K+ balance via Mercury Advisory
Mercury Plus/Pro are flat monthly fees, not per-user
Airwallex charges 2.80%-4.30% + $0.30 per transaction; Mercury's core banking is fee-free

faqs.question: Does Airwallex charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Yes — 2.80% + $0.30 for domestic cards and 4.30% + $0.30 for international cards, per Airwallex's pricing page.

faqs.question: Is Mercury free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mercury's core business banking plan is $0/month with no minimum balance or overdraft fees.

faqs.question: How many currencies does Airwallex support?
faqs.answer: Airwallex offers global accounts in 20+ currencies and transfers to 200+ countries.

faqs.question: What AI features does Mercury offer?
faqs.answer: Mercury uses AI to read and populate bill details and to categorize transactions automatically.

faqs.question: What does Airwallex's Grow plan add?
faqs.answer: Grow adds advanced approval workflows, an AI expense policy agent, bill payment automation, and NetSuite/Dynamics 365 integration for $12/user/month plus a platform fee.

faqs.question: How many businesses use each platform?
faqs.answer: Airwallex states it's used by 200,000+ businesses including Shein and Canva; Mercury states it's trusted by 300,000+ entrepreneurs processing $20B+ in monthly transaction volume.

content.verdict: Moss offers a genuine free tier for small teams (up to 3 users, 20 invoices/month) before moving to custom quote-based pricing, while Spendesk skips the free tier entirely and is quote-based from the first plan (Foundations) but bundles unlimited cards and users plus a free CFO Connect Pro membership. Both are AI-assisted, European spend management platforms — the difference is where in the buying journey pricing transparency disappears.
content.bestForToolA: Small teams (up to 3 users) in Germany or the wider DACH region who want to try spend management for free — with BaFin-regulated banking and DATEV integration — before committing to a paid plan.
content.bestForToolB: Larger finance teams that want unlimited cards and users from day one, need SAP integration, and are willing to go through custom quoting in exchange for a bundled CFO Connect Pro membership (normally €625).
content.whoNeedsBoth: A multi-entity company with a German-regulated subsidiary (fitting Moss's BaFin oversight and DATEV integration) alongside a larger parent entity running SAP may end up running Moss and Spendesk side by side for different legal entities.

keyDifferences.title: Free tier availability
keyDifferences.toolA: Free plan for up to 3 users with unlimited cards and up to 20 invoices/month.
keyDifferences.toolB: No free plan or free trial advertised — pricing_model is Custom/Enterprise from the start.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams wanting to test spend management without a sales conversation can only do so with Moss.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Small businesses and startups evaluating spend management tools on a budget.

keyDifferences.title: Bundled perks
keyDifferences.toolA: No bundled membership perk is mentioned in the facts.
keyDifferences.toolB: Includes a free CFO Connect Pro membership (normally €625) for customers.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: This is a concrete monetary perk that offsets Spendesk's lack of a free tier.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Finance leaders who value community/network access alongside the software.

keyDifferences.title: AI automation approach
keyDifferences.toolA: AI Pre-Accounting Agent automates expense coding and invoice processing, offered as an add-on on paid plans.
keyDifferences.toolB: Spendesk AI Connect reads documents, suggests bookkeeping entries, and flags duplicates and anomalies, included as part of the Foundations platform.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Whether AI accounting automation is bundled into the base plan or sold as an add-on affects total cost.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Finance teams comparing all-in cost of AI-assisted bookkeeping.

keyDifferences.title: Regulatory framing
keyDifferences.toolA: Operated by Nufin GmbH and regulated by BaFin (Germany's financial supervisory authority) as an e-money institution.
keyDifferences.toolB: Described as "built, regulated, and profitable in Europe" without naming a specific regulator or headquarters city.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Companies with compliance requirements tied to a specific regulator may need the named oversight Moss provides.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Compliance and finance teams needing to document a vendor's regulatory status.

keyDifferences.title: ERP/accounting integration depth
keyDifferences.toolA: 40+ integrations including Xero, NetSuite, Dynamics, DATEV, Deel, Personio, and Workday.
keyDifferences.toolB: Integrates with Sage, Xero, NetSuite, SAP, and Datev.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: SAP support is unique to Spendesk in these facts, which matters for larger enterprises already on SAP.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Enterprises with existing SAP or HR-system investments (Personio, Workday) choosing based on their stack.

rows.note: Moss free plan capped at 3 users, 20 invoices/month
Moss's AI is a paid add-on; Spendesk's AI Connect is part of Foundations
Moss: 40+ incl. DATEV; Spendesk: Sage, Xero, NetSuite, SAP, Datev
Moss supports 70+ currencies

faqs.question: Does Moss have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Moss offers a free plan for up to 3 users with unlimited cards and up to 20 invoices per month.

faqs.question: Does Spendesk have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Spendesk doesn't publish fixed prices or a free plan — it uses a fixed platform fee plus variable fees based on transaction volume, available only via a custom quote.

faqs.question: What does Moss's AI do?
faqs.answer: Moss uses an AI-powered pre-accounting agent to automate invoice coding and expense processing.

faqs.question: Does Spendesk use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Spendesk uses AI to read documents, suggest bookkeeping entries, detect duplicates, and flag anomalies, plus a Spendesk AI Connect integration.

faqs.question: Who regulates Moss?
faqs.answer: Moss is operated by Nufin GmbH and regulated by BaFin, Germany's financial supervisory authority, as an e-money institution.

faqs.question: What's included in Spendesk's Foundations plan?
faqs.answer: Foundations includes unlimited physical and virtual cards, unlimited users, receipt OCR automation, expense tracking, budget monitoring, and API access.

content.verdict: Novo is entirely free with a single checking account plan, AI-assisted onboarding and categorization, and an option for business loans. Relay is freemium: its free Starter plan already includes up to 20 checking sub-accounts, but higher savings APY (up to 3.00%) and automation like recurring invoices only unlock on the paid Grow ($30/month) or Scale ($90/month) tiers. The decision hinges on whether a business needs one simple account or several segregated sub-accounts with tiered yield.
content.bestForToolA: Solo founders and small businesses who want one free checking account, AI-assisted expense categorization, and access to business loan options without any monthly fees.
content.bestForToolB: Businesses that want to split funds across multiple checking sub-accounts (e.g., taxes, payroll, operating) with auto-transfer rules and are willing to pay for Grow or Scale to unlock higher savings APY and bookkeeping automation.
content.whoNeedsBoth: A business could use Novo as a simple free operating account for day-to-day transactions while running Relay's multi-account structure for allocating and reserving funds — though most businesses evaluating these will pick one primary banking relationship.

keyDifferences.title: Number of checking accounts
keyDifferences.toolA: No sub-account structure is documented — Novo is presented as a single business checking account.
keyDifferences.toolB: Up to 20 checking accounts on the free Starter plan (50 on Scale) with auto-transfer rules between them.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses that need to separate funds for taxes, payroll, or specific projects benefit from Relay's built-in multi-account structure.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Businesses managing multiple budget categories or entities from one banking relationship.

keyDifferences.title: High-yield savings
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented — no APY or savings yield is mentioned for Novo.
keyDifferences.toolB: APY ranges from 1.11% on the free Starter plan up to 3.00% on the Scale plan.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses holding cash reserves may prefer a bank that pays yield on savings balances.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Businesses with idle cash reserves looking to earn yield without moving to a separate treasury product.

keyDifferences.title: AI-assisted banking
keyDifferences.toolA: AI-assisted onboarding and expense categorization, powered by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini.
keyDifferences.toolB: No AI capability is mentioned in the facts.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses wanting automated bookkeeping help out of the box may lean toward Novo's AI features.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Small business owners without dedicated bookkeeping staff.

keyDifferences.title: Business loans
keyDifferences.toolA: Offers business loan options, though rates and terms aren't published publicly.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — no lending product is mentioned.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: A business seeking financing alongside banking has one fewer vendor relationship to manage with Novo.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Small businesses that may need working capital financing.

keyDifferences.title: Cashback rewards
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented in the facts.
keyDifferences.toolB: Tiered cashback on credit cards: 1% (Starter), 1.25% (Grow), 1.5% (Scale).
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses with significant card spend can factor cashback into total cost of banking.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Businesses that put substantial recurring spend on a company card.

rows.note: Relay: up to 20 free, 50 on Scale
1.11%-3.00% APY depending on plan
1%-1.5% tiered by plan
Novo's AI is powered by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini
Relay names Xero, QuickBooks Online, Gusto; Novo doesn't name specific integrations
Relay offers a 14-day free trial on Grow and Scale
Novo has a single free plan; Relay has Grow ($30/mo) and Scale ($90/mo)

faqs.question: Is Novo really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Novo advertises free business checking with no monthly fees for the core account.

faqs.question: Is Relay free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Relay's Starter plan is free and includes up to 20 checking accounts and 1.11% APY on savings.

faqs.question: Does Novo use AI?
faqs.answer: Novo states it uses AI powered by Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini for onboarding and expense categorization.

faqs.question: How many checking accounts does Relay support?
faqs.answer: Up to 20 on the free Starter plan and up to 50 on the Scale plan.

faqs.question: What APY does Relay offer?
faqs.answer: 1.11% APY on the free Starter plan, rising to 1.75% on Grow and 3.00% on Scale.

faqs.question: Who holds Novo deposits?
faqs.answer: Novo banking services are provided by Middlesex Federal Savings, F.A., a Member FDIC bank.

content.verdict: Feathery is purpose-built for financial services workflows — document intelligence, e-signatures, and HIPAA compliance — but its Growth and Business tiers are entirely quote-based. Fillout is a general-purpose form/quiz/survey builder with published fixed pricing ($15/$40/$75/month) and no per-seat charges on any plan, but it doesn't offer Feathery's document intelligence or HIPAA compliance. The right fit depends on whether the use case is regulated financial workflows or general-purpose form building.
content.bestForToolA: Financial services companies — registered investment advisors, broker-dealers, banks, insurance carriers and brokers — that need document intelligence, e-signatures, and HIPAA-eligible compliance for client onboarding workflows.
content.bestForToolB: Teams that want a general-purpose form, quiz, or survey builder with transparent, fixed-price tiers and no per-seat charges, even as they add team members.
content.whoNeedsBoth: A fintech company might use Fillout for public-facing marketing surveys and lead-gen forms while using Feathery for regulated client onboarding, document intelligence, and e-signature workflows in its back office.

keyDifferences.title: Industry focus
keyDifferences.toolA: Positioned specifically for financial services — RIAs, broker-dealers, banks, insurance carriers and brokers.
keyDifferences.toolB: General-purpose form, quiz, and survey builder with no stated industry focus.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Feathery's feature set (document intelligence, HIPAA) is tailored to regulated workflows that Fillout doesn't target.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Compliance and operations teams in regulated financial industries versus general marketing/ops teams.

keyDifferences.title: Document intelligence & e-signatures
keyDifferences.toolA: Document Templates & eSignatures plus Document Intelligence extracting structured data at a stated 98%+ accuracy.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — Fillout's facts describe form/quiz/survey building, not document extraction or e-signatures.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Workflows requiring signed documents and automated data extraction need Feathery's capabilities.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Teams handling contracts, applications, or onboarding paperwork.

keyDifferences.title: Pricing transparency
keyDifferences.toolA: Growth and Business plans are custom, quote-based — no published prices beyond the free tier.
keyDifferences.toolB: Starter ($15/mo), Pro ($40/mo), and Business ($75/mo) all have published fixed prices.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams that need to budget without a sales call will find Fillout's pricing easier to evaluate upfront.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Self-serve buyers and smaller teams without a procurement process.

keyDifferences.title: HIPAA compliance
keyDifferences.toolA: Growth/Business plans include HIPAA and GDPR compliance.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — no HIPAA compliance is mentioned.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Healthcare-adjacent financial workflows (e.g., insurance intake) may require HIPAA-eligible tooling.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Insurance and healthcare-adjacent finance teams.

keyDifferences.title: Per-seat charges
keyDifferences.toolA: Free plan includes unlimited seats; paid tier seat costs aren't detailed.
keyDifferences.toolB: Explicitly states no per-seat charges on any plan.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Growing teams want certainty that adding users won't increase cost.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Teams anticipating headcount growth.

rows.note: Feathery emphasizes pixel-perfect forms but doesn't use the term drag-and-drop
Feathery's Robin builds/deploys workflows from natural language; Fillout offers AI form/quiz/survey creation plus Google Forms import
Both gate SSO to a custom-quote tier

rows.feature: Published fixed pricing on paid tiers
rows.note: Feathery's Growth/Business are quote-based; Fillout publishes $15/$40/$75
Feathery: 500/month; Fillout: 1,000/month

faqs.question: Does Feathery have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes unlimited questions, seats, pages, and conditions, with 500 monthly submissions and 5 live forms.

faqs.question: Does Fillout have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fillout's Free plan includes 1,000 responses/month with unlimited forms, seats, and questions.

faqs.question: Is Feathery's paid pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, the Growth and Business plans require contacting sales for a custom quote.

faqs.question: What is Fillout's starting paid price?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $15/month ($180/year billed annually) for 2,000 responses/month.

faqs.question: Does Feathery support e-signatures?
faqs.answer: Yes, through its Document Templates & eSignatures feature.

faqs.question: Does Fillout charge per seat?
faqs.answer: No, Fillout states there are no per-seat charges on any plan.

content.verdict: Formbricks is an open-source, self-hostable survey and experience-management platform with AI labeling/translation, entering paid tiers at $74/month. Formsort is a low-code, multi-step form builder aimed at product teams — with built-in A/B testing, data warehouse integrations, and HIPAA compliance for healthcare intake — entering paid tiers at a steeper $279/month. They solve different problems: feedback collection versus structured, conversion-optimized form flows.
content.bestForToolA: Teams that want an open-source, self-hostable survey platform for multi-channel feedback (web, email, in-app) with AI-assisted labeling and translation, at a lower paid entry price.
content.bestForToolB: Product teams building multi-step, data-driven forms — especially healthcare intake requiring HIPAA and a BAA — that need built-in A/B testing and integrations with BigQuery, Redshift, Postgres, Salesforce, Segment, or Amplitude.
content.whoNeedsBoth: A healthcare or fintech product organization might run Formsort for regulated, HIPAA-eligible intake and onboarding flows while using Formbricks separately to collect NPS and in-app feedback surveys from existing customers.

keyDifferences.title: Open source and self-hosting
keyDifferences.toolA: Fully open source with source code on GitHub and a self-hosting option instead of the cloud service.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — no mention of open source or self-hosting.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams with data residency or vendor lock-in concerns may require the ability to self-host.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Engineering-led teams and companies with strict data governance requirements.

keyDifferences.title: HIPAA compliance
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented — no HIPAA mention in the facts.
keyDifferences.toolB: HIPAA compliance with a signed BAA available (Enterprise plan).
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Healthcare intake workflows legally require HIPAA-eligible tooling, which only Formsort offers here.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Healthcare and insurance product teams collecting patient information.

keyDifferences.title: AI capabilities
keyDifferences.toolA: AI-based feedback labeling and AI translations, available on Scale/Pro plans.
keyDifferences.toolB: Explicitly states no AI capabilities are mentioned on the site.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams wanting automated categorization of open-text survey responses or multi-language support get that only from Formbricks.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Global teams running multi-language surveys or high response volumes needing automated tagging.

keyDifferences.title: Entry-level paid pricing
keyDifferences.toolA: Pro plan starts at $74/month.
keyDifferences.toolB: Pro plan starts at $279/month.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: The price gap is substantial once free-tier volume is exceeded, affecting budget-conscious teams differently.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Smaller teams and startups sensitive to software spend.

keyDifferences.title: Data warehouse and A/B testing integrations
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented — no data warehouse or A/B testing integrations listed.
keyDifferences.toolB: Native integrations with BigQuery, Postgres, Redshift, Salesforce, Segment, Google Analytics, Optimizely, and Amplitude, plus built-in A/B testing.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Product and growth teams optimizing conversion need direct pipelines into analytics and warehouse tools.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Growth and data teams running conversion experiments on forms.

rows.note: Formsort offers a BAA on its Enterprise plan
Formsort: BigQuery, Postgres, Redshift
Formsort's facts state no AI capabilities are mentioned on the site
Formbricks: 250 responses/month; Formsort: 100 flow starts/month
Formbricks Pro $74/month vs. Formsort Pro $279/month
Formbricks' highest published tier is Scale ($325/mo); Formsort has a separate quote-based Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Is Formbricks open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Formbricks is fully open source with code available on GitHub, and it can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does Formsort have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports unlimited users and forms with 100 flow starts/month, though it includes Formsort branding.

faqs.question: Is Formsort HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Formsort's Enterprise plan includes HIPAA compliance with a BAA.

faqs.question: Does Formbricks offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro plan includes AI translations and the Scale plan adds AI feedback labeling.

faqs.question: What integrations does Formsort support?
faqs.answer: BigQuery, PostgreSQL, Redshift, Salesforce, Google Sheets, webhooks, Segment, Google Analytics, Optimizely, and Amplitude.

faqs.question: How do Formbricks and Formsort compare on entry paid pricing?
faqs.answer: Formbricks Pro starts at $74/month, while Formsort Pro starts at $279/month.

content.verdict: Heyflow is a paid-only, no-code funnel builder built around native server-side ad tracking (Meta, TikTok, Google, LinkedIn, Bing), phone-level lead validation, and in-flow payments and booking — starting at $45/month with no free plan. Outgrow is a freemium platform spanning quizzes, calculators, chatbots, and assessments with 1000+ templates, starting free and scaling through published tiers from $14/month. The tradeoff is tracking/conversion depth versus content-type breadth and pricing transparency.
content.bestForToolA: Performance marketers running paid campaigns who need native server-side conversion tracking across major ad platforms and want to collect Stripe payments, e-signatures, or bookings inside the same funnel.
content.bestForToolB: Teams that want to start free and build a wide range of interactive content — quizzes, calculators, chatbots, assessments — from a large template library, scaling through clearly published monthly tiers.
content.whoNeedsBoth: An agency running paid-ad lead-gen funnels for clients may use Heyflow for tracking-heavy ad campaigns while using Outgrow to build calculators and assessments for organic and content-marketing lead generation.

keyDifferences.title: Native ad tracking
keyDifferences.toolA: Built-in server-side tracking for Meta CAPI, TikTok, Google Ads, LinkedIn, and Bing.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — no ad-platform tracking integrations are mentioned.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Paid-media teams need accurate conversion tracking as browser-side tracking degrades; only Heyflow addresses this directly.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Performance marketing and growth teams running paid acquisition.

keyDifferences.title: Phone-level lead validation
keyDifferences.toolA: HLR and SMS OTP verification of phone numbers submitted through a flow.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses paying for leads by volume need to filter out invalid phone numbers before they're billed or routed to sales.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Lead-gen businesses in verticals like insurance, home services, or finance.

keyDifferences.title: Free plan availability
keyDifferences.toolA: No free plan — pricing_model is Paid, starting at $45/month (a free test is mentioned but no ongoing free tier).
keyDifferences.toolB: Free Forms/Surveys tier with 100 leads/month and 4 content pieces, no credit card required.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams wanting to trial the product without payment can only do so on Outgrow.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Budget-constrained teams and solo marketers evaluating tools before committing spend.

keyDifferences.title: Content-type breadth
keyDifferences.toolA: Focused on interactive multi-step funnels and lead-gen forms.
keyDifferences.toolB: Supports quizzes, calculators, chatbots, surveys, polls, forms, and assessments across 1000+ templates.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams needing a wide range of interactive content formats beyond lead-gen funnels get more variety from Outgrow.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Content marketing teams building varied interactive experiences.

keyDifferences.title: In-flow payments and booking
keyDifferences.toolA: Collects Stripe payments, e-signatures, and Calendly-style appointment bookings without leaving the funnel.
keyDifferences.toolB: Native integrations connect to payment platforms, but in-flow e-signatures and booking aren't described.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses that want to close a transaction (payment, signed agreement, booked call) in one flow need Heyflow's combined feature set.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Sales-led businesses converting leads directly into paid or booked outcomes.

rows.note: Outgrow offers 1000+ templates
Heyflow is focused on funnels/forms rather than the broader content types Outgrow supports
Outgrow's native integrations connect to payment platforms but don't describe in-flow e-signatures/booking
Heyflow: free test per FAQ; Outgrow: 7-day Business trial, no credit card

faqs.question: Does Heyflow have a free plan?
faqs.answer: There's no ongoing free plan; a free test is available and paid plans start from $45/month.

faqs.question: Does Outgrow have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Forms/Surveys tier is free with 100 leads/month and 4 content pieces.

faqs.question: Does Heyflow support ad tracking?
faqs.answer: Yes, native server-side tracking for Meta CAPI, TikTok, Google Ads, LinkedIn, and Bing.

faqs.question: Can Heyflow collect payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, Heyflow supports Stripe payments, e-signatures, and appointment booking directly within a funnel.

faqs.question: Where is Outgrow based?
faqs.answer: Outgrow lists its headquarters in Florida, USA.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Outgrow?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day trial of the Business plan is offered without requiring a credit card.

content.verdict: involve.me combines native payment collection (Stripe, PayPal, Mollie), a built-in CRM, and 55+ integrations into interactive funnels starting at $19/month. ScoreApp focuses on AI-generated scorecards and quizzes with personalized dynamic results, deep integration into external CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce, Keap), and a 14-day money-back guarantee, starting at $39/month. The choice depends on whether payments need to happen inside the funnel and whether a built-in CRM or integration with an existing one is preferred.
content.bestForToolA: Teams wanting to collect payments directly inside interactive funnels and consolidate lead scoring, email automation, and CRM functions in one platform without a separate CRM subscription.
content.bestForToolB: Coaches and consultants building lead-qualifying scorecards or assessments who already run an external CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, Keap, Insightly, Mailchimp, or Kit) and want a money-back guarantee on paid plans.
content.whoNeedsBoth: An agency serving multiple clients might use involve.me for a client that wants payment-collecting funnels and a self-contained CRM, while using ScoreApp for a different client whose lead-scoring quiz needs to plug into an existing HubSpot or Salesforce instance.

keyDifferences.title: Native payment collection
keyDifferences.toolA: Accepts payments directly in funnels via Stripe, PayPal, or Mollie.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not documented — no payment collection feature is mentioned.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses that want to sell directly through a quiz or funnel need native payments, which only involve.me documents.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: E-commerce and service businesses selling through interactive funnels.

keyDifferences.title: Built-in CRM vs. external CRM integrations
keyDifferences.toolA: Includes a built-in CRM storing contact records with mapped properties and dynamic segments.
keyDifferences.toolB: Integrates with external CRMs — HubSpot, Salesforce, Keap, Insightly, Mailchimp, and Kit — rather than offering its own.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams without an existing CRM may prefer involve.me's all-in-one approach, while teams with an established CRM stack may prefer ScoreApp's integration-first model.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Small teams without a CRM versus organizations already invested in a specific CRM.

keyDifferences.title: Entry-level pricing
keyDifferences.toolA: Start plan begins at $19/month.
keyDifferences.toolB: Starter plan begins at $39/month.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: involve.me's lower entry price may suit budget-conscious teams testing paid features.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Smaller businesses and solo operators evaluating cost.

keyDifferences.title: Free tier limits
keyDifferences.toolA: Free plan allows 50 submissions or 500 visits per month with no credit card required.
keyDifferences.toolB: Free plan is capped at 1 live scorecard and 10 responses per month.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: involve.me's free tier supports meaningfully higher volume for testing before upgrading.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Teams wanting to validate a funnel or quiz at higher volume before paying.

keyDifferences.title: Money-back guarantee
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented — no guarantee is mentioned.
keyDifferences.toolB: Offers a 14-day money-back guarantee on paid plans.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: A stated guarantee reduces the risk of committing to a paid plan.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Buyers who want a safety net before committing budget.

faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan offers 1 live scorecard and up to 10 responses per month.

content.verdict: Formcarry runs on recurring monthly subscriptions ($5-$80/month, billed annually) with team-member caps that grow by tier and a custom Enterprise SLA option. Formspark instead sells one-time, non-expiring data bundles — pay once for a pool of submissions rather than a recurring plan — with unlimited team members on every tier but no Enterprise option. The right pick depends on whether predictable recurring pricing or a pay-once model with unlimited seats matters more.
content.bestForToolA: Teams that want predictable monthly recurring pricing with tiers based on team size and, at the top end, a dedicated Enterprise plan with a 99.9% uptime SLA.
content.bestForToolB: Developers and small teams that prefer paying once for a non-expiring pool of submissions instead of a subscription, need unlimited team members from the free tier, and want broad framework support (React, Next.js, Webflow, static site generators).
content.whoNeedsBoth: An agency managing many small client sites might use Formspark's low-cost one-time bundles for low-traffic static sites while using Formcarry's subscription tiers — with more team members and an Enterprise SLA — for higher-volume client projects needing guaranteed uptime.

keyDifferences.title: Subscription vs. one-time pricing
keyDifferences.toolA: Recurring monthly plans (Starter $5/mo, Basic $15/mo, Premium $80/mo, billed annually) with submission caps that reset monthly.
keyDifferences.toolB: One-time, non-expiring data bundles (e.g., $25 for 50,000 submissions and 100 forms) instead of a recurring subscription.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses with steady, predictable form volume may prefer a subscription; those with sporadic or one-off projects may prefer paying once.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Recurring SaaS-style buyers versus freelancers or agencies running one-off client sites.

keyDifferences.title: Team member limits
keyDifferences.toolA: Team members are capped per plan: 1 (BABY), 3 (Starter), 5 (Basic), 20 (Premium).
keyDifferences.toolB: Unlimited team members on every plan, including the Free tier.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Larger or distributed teams that need many collaborators without upgrading purely for seat count benefit from Formspark's model.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Larger teams or agencies collaborating across many contributors.

keyDifferences.title: Multi-language support
keyDifferences.toolA: Not documented — no multi-language support is mentioned.
keyDifferences.toolB: Supports 10 languages, including English, Spanish, French, German, and Ukrainian.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Sites serving non-English-speaking users need localized thank-you pages, emails, and error messages.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: International or multi-language websites.

keyDifferences.title: Framework/platform support
keyDifferences.toolA: Works with plain HTML, jQuery, React, or Vue forms.
keyDifferences.toolB: Supports 20+ frontend platforms including React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Svelte, WordPress, Webflow, Hugo, Jekyll, and Astro.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Teams on modern frameworks or static site generators get broader native support from Formspark.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Developers using static site generators or newer frontend frameworks.

keyDifferences.title: Enterprise option
keyDifferences.toolA: Custom Enterprise plan with a 99.9% uptime SLA and unlimited submissions/uploads.
keyDifferences.toolB: Not offered — only Free and one Upgrade bundle are available.
keyDifferences.whyItMatters: Businesses needing an SLA guarantee for high-volume, mission-critical forms only have that option with Formcarry.
keyDifferences.benefitsWho: Larger businesses with uptime and support requirements.

rows.note: Formcarry: built-in, 98.6% stated accuracy; Formspark: built-in plus optional Botpoison/reCAPTCHA/hCaptcha/Turnstile

faqs.question: Does Formcarry have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the BABY plan is free and includes 1 form and 50 submissions/month.

faqs.question: Does Formspark have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, every account includes a free tier with 250 submissions and 10 forms.

faqs.question: How is Formcarry priced?
faqs.answer: Paid plans (Starter $5/mo, Basic $15/mo, Premium $80/mo) are billed annually, with a custom Enterprise option available.

faqs.question: How does Formspark pricing work?
faqs.answer: Formspark uses one-time, non-expiring data bundles (e.g. $25 for 50,000 submissions and 100 forms) rather than recurring subscriptions.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Formcarry support?
faqs.answer: Formcarry works with plain HTML, jQuery, React, and Vue forms.

faqs.question: What platforms does Formspark support?
faqs.answer: Formspark supports 20+ platforms including React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Svelte, WordPress, Webflow, Hugo, Jekyll, and Astro.
